title: The Renaissance in Venice in the 1500s
topic_path: The Renaissance in Venice in the 1500s

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the most important factor contributing to Venice's wealth?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Venice was wealthy from trade and served as a gateway between Europe and the East.
[Read this article to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1500-1600-end-of-the-renaissance-and-the-reformation/venice-1/a/introduction)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Veronese, and Venetian painters generally, are known for their . . .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Veronese, and Venetian painters generally, are known for their sophisticated use of color.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The source for Correggio's Jupiter and Io was a . . .**
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3d58e301a4ad5055b656c6b351109b49.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The subject matter comes from the poetry of Ovid, the classical Roman writer, which was being revived at this time.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Titian created an asymmetrical composition, that $\_\_\_\_\_\_$.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2b5c5ac8e4a8d956e4907aba1fac9137.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Titian created an asymmetrical composition, that was unusual for that time and anticipated Mannerism.

choices.content: looked back to Early Renaissance painting

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is true of Titian's *Venus of Urbino*?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/eb2b58f7f927e0917e219fc024c4291d.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Titian used glazes, or thin layers of translucent oil paint, to create this painting and especially the glowing nude. He manipulated the proportions of the body to make her extra elegant. Titian was looking back to Giorgione's reclining nude (*Sleeping Venus*) for inspiration. With this painting, Titian establishes the genre of the reclining female nude that many later artists used.

He did not title the painting, and this is not an allegory of chaste love.

choices.content: Titian used glazes, or thin layers of translucent oil paint, to create this painting and especially the glowing nude.
Titian manipulated the proportions of the body to make her extra elegant.
Titian was looking back to Giorgione's reclining nude (*Sleeping Venus*) for inspiration
With this painting, Titian establishes the genre of the reclining female nude that many later artists will use.
Titian himself titled the painting *Venus of Urbino.*
This painting is an allegory of chaste love.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Giorgione used oil paint in a different way than Bellini by emphasizing . . .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Giorgione used oil paint in a different way than Bellini by emphasizing the opacity of the paint instead of using transparent glazes.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Giorgione's *Adoration of the Shepherds* broke with tradition by  . . .**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c8eb4fb5b79005e1758a700e27d45113.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This version of the Adoration of the Shepherds broke with tradition by not employing symmetry and a visual hierarchy that emphasized the Madonna and Child.

choices.content: not employing symmetry and a visual hierarchy that emphasized the Madonna and Child.
including a landscape that used atmospheric perspective to show a deep space.
showing the holy family against a dark cave.
setting the Holy Family in an idealized landscape

assessment_items.question_content:
**What traditional features does Tintoretto revive in the *Last Supper*?**
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f1ce72b5b5c33fbf7bd697071516e73b.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Tintoretto returns to the spiritual symbolism of haloed and other markers of divinity that were largely abandoned by artists like Leonardo and Raphael in the High Renaissance.

choices.content: halos and other markers of divinity.

hints: Tintoretto's style can be identified by Mannerist characteristics such as a compositional complexity (so that it can be hard to understand what's happening), distorted—almost dance-like—poses, and extreme foreshortening. Tintoretto is also known for extreme contrasts of light and shadow, brilliant color and quick, visible brushstrokes.

choices.content: Veronese, *Feast in the House of Levi*, 1573

assessment_items.question_content:
The original title for this painting was the *Last Supper*.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6367607282d6f21f06d2d3671de6aafd.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Veronese was called to testify before the inquisition in 1573, since there was concern that the painting lacked adequate seriousness and piety. Instead of changing the paining, the artist simply changed the title from the Last Supper to Feast in the House of Levi.
